title: Volodymyr Zelensky: 'The pressure on Russia is insufficient. Not only from the US, but also from Europe'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president expressed concern about the consequences of the war in the Middle East on the conflict between Kyiv and Moscow. Pressured by both the Americans and the Russians to give up the Donbas, he defended the region's strategic importance and warned Europeans of the urgent need to lift the Hungarian veto on the €90 billion loan approved in 2025.

Pierre Buhler, former diplomat: We are witnessing 'a worrying crack in the European edifice'
subhead: The former ambassador highlights the divisions among European Union member states over how to approach relations with the US and warns of the dangers of what he calls a worrying 'Faustian bargain' between Europe and Donald Trump.

title: EU experts back broad 'forever chemicals' ban
subhead: A highly anticipated report from scientific advisers to the European Union finds that PFAS present 'growing risks' to the population and the environment and should face broad limits across Europe.

title: The sensuality of Henri Rousseau's paintings at the Musée de l'Orangerie
subhead: The exhibition 'Henri Rousseau: A Painter's Ambition' offers a stimulating reinterpretation of the artist's work, highlighting his rigor and discipline, far removed from the cliché of the 'naïve' or 'Sunday painter.'

title: Vote transfers between France's right and far right are increasingly common
subhead: By alternately voting for either the far right or the mainstream right to defeat the left, voters in the recent municipal elections have illustrated the two parties' ideological convergence.
